info.title: Generic Maven dependency API transformation rule for license-maven-plugin

parts.text: "Project @license-maven-plugin/ does not compile due to a breaking dependency update. Your goal is to generate a GENERIC, REUSABLE transformation rule - not a one-off patch - that can be applied to ANY Maven project affected by the same breaking change. Plan: 1) Identify the compilation errors in @license-maven-plugin/ and determine which API    (class/method/constructor/signature) changed in the dependency.    The new dependency API specification is available at /workspace/maven-dependency-tree-3.1.0-api.md and the Javadoc at /workspace/javadoc-maven-dependency-tree-3.1.0/    for reference when identifying replacement types, methods, or fields. 2) Characterize the breaking change abstractly:    - What was the old API pattern? (e.g., Foo.bar(String))    - What is the new API pattern? (e.g., Foo.bar(String, boolean))    - What structural transformation does this require at the call site?    Do NOT assume the fix is specific to this project - any project calling the old API will need the same transformation. 3) Generate a GENERIC JavaParser transformation using the template in @/workspace/javaparser-base-template/:    - Match the old API pattern structurally, not by project-specific class names.    - Use patterns to traverse all files and apply the fix wherever the old pattern appears.    - Parameterize by fully-qualified type names and method signatures from the dependency, NOT from the client.    - Save the transformation in /workspace/javaparser-base-template/src/main/java/github/chains/Main.java.    - Use the JavaParser API documentation located in folder /workspace/javaparser-api-docs/ for reference. 4) Compile and validate the transformation to ensure it has no errors. 5) Execute the transformation to @license-maven-plugin/ and ensure both compilation errors and test errors are fixed. 6) Ensure generalizability: ensure the generated rule contains no hardcoded project-specific identifiers.    The rule must be applicable to other projects by simply changing the input source directory path."

**Modifying code structure**

I need to modify the code in the /workspace/javaparser-base-template, and it looks like I might have to keep things separate. First, I'll inspect the repository structure. It’s possible I’ll have to use TODO comments to keep track, which can get a bit complex. I should identify any compilation errors in the license-maven-plugin due to a dependency update. After that, I’ll create a generic transformation rule in template Main.java and validate the compilation. I might even need to run transformations on the project and tests. Time to check the workspace!
I’m going to inspect the failing project, the dependency API docs, and the JavaParser template to identify the breaking API change before writing a generic rewrite rule.

128:     final ConcurrentMap<License, Set<Artifact>> map = new ConcurrentHashMap<License, Set<Artifact>>();

130:     // license:artifact is a many-to-many relationship.
131:     // Each artifact may have several licenses.
132:     // Each artifact may appear multiple times in the map.
133:     dependencies.parallelStream().forEach(artifact -> getLicensesFromArtifact(artifact).forEach(license -> {
